programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.sub-title: Handlebar, Casa Roma and The Black Pearl

programme.title: Car S.O.S
programme.desc: Tim and Fuzz take on a tatty 1986 Rover SD1. The car was once the pride and joy of gardener Darren, who won awards with it at car shows.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike, Frank, Danielle and Robbie continue to pick their way through Virginia. But they hit a roadblock when a seller wont let go of a one-of-a-kind item.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, Henry Cole and Simon OBrien meet farmer Ken. In his barns, Henry finds a rare pre-WWII exercise bike and farmyard scales that were used to weigh potatoes.

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Win the Dream
programme.desc: Nick and Nicole from Chateau DIY join Max to judge the business-plan builds. But with only two places available to progress, the pressure becomes too much for some...

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Angela and Simon dream of owning a farmhouse in Frances Poitou-Charentes region, where they can indulge in art and yoga. Lucy Alexander shows them what £225k can buy.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Marcia wants a holiday home in the Gran Alacant area of Spains Costa Blanca for her £110k. Will a spacious flat hitting all the right notes or a tempting town house be the perfect one?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in York is motivational master and training guru Steve Carlyle, whose `sophisticated dinner party, complete with butler and posh nosh, does anything but boost his reputation.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day two in York, and eccentric ghost tour walker Trevor Rooney throws a `Fright Night themed evening in a haunted bric-a-brac warehouse.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Next to host in York is hippy hostess Annie Albericci who hopes to bring peace, love and harmony to the table with her `festival chic themed evening, but Steve Carlyle threatens to ruin the mood.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pint-sized nurse Yvonne McGill hosts a `black and white swinging 60s dinner party on day four in York but worries that straight-talking Steve will ruin the evening for everyone.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its a night of flirting and friction on the final day of the competition in York, as foodie perfectionist Rebecca Ryan throws a Hollywood themed evening of glitz and glamour, with some help from `Peggy and `Audrey, her kitchen appliances.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Candy and Mark are looking for the perfect rural retreat to escape to in Almería, southern Spain. Ben Hillman lines up five fab options for £75k, but will any of them result in an offer?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil revisit two sets fussy buyers looking for family homes at opposite ends of the country. Ian and his family were on the hunt for the perfect seaside home in the pricey coastal town of Broadstairs in Kent. Meanwhile, soon-to-be-married couple Tara and Alastair were desperate to buy their first home together in Edinburgh, with a healthy budget of £600k.

programme.title: Roman Empire by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: Alice embarks on her most ambitious journey to date in search of the Roman Empire, by train, through Italy, France and Spain seeking the secrets of Romes success.

programme.title: Sailing the Shipping Forecast with the Rev Richard Coles
programme.desc: Richard enjoys a Wight good time at Cowes Week racing regatta and tests out his seven-star service skills at a superyacht school.

programme.title: Night Coppers
programme.desc: Sam and Ed are called to protect the public from a gas leak at a petrol station. Zoe and Emma respond to a domestic dispute about missing teeth. And Sam helps a security guard whos felt the full force of a disgruntled drunk in his nether regions.

programme.title: Joe Lycett: Im About to Lose Control And I Think Joe Lycett
programme.desc: Joe shares jokes, paintings and some of the trolling hes been up to, such as breaking into banks, selling art for 12.5 million pounds and taking on bronze medal winning Olympians.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: In Nottingham, staff at Queens Medical Centre deal with a crime wave of needle-spiking attacks on female university students - including Kate, who collapsed at a nightclub.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Ramsay urges two brothers to work through their emotional conflicts in order to save their beloved Bel Aire Diner in Astoria, New York.

programme.title: Trucking Heavy

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dog handler Darren calls on all his powers of persuasion to try and end a rooftop stand-off. Steve is forced to use his pepper spray when a suspect refuses to come quietly, and tasers are drawn in a situation with a man who is armed with a knife.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Paul takes the lead in a dangerous high-speed pursuit through the streets of Stockton, Justin is put in severe danger by a man who has allegedly been threatening the public with a dirty needle, and Damo leads the firearms team on a raid of a house.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: A shocking hit and run leaves an elderly driver with serious head injuries, and a rude drivers outburst against police officers brings him under scrutiny which he could live to regret.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: Humber Bridge staff alert police to a car has just passed through stinking of cannabis. And in Grimsby, a dog unit chases a car that fails to stop.

programme.title: Police: Suspect No. 1
programme.desc: Victims are terrorised in their home by a knife-wielding intruder, twice in 24 hours. The race is on for police to catch the hardened criminal and his accomplice before another strike.

programme.title: Banged up Abroad
programme.desc: Dramatic re-creations of sojourns in paradise that ended in brutal foreign jails.

programme.title: Extreme Tow Truckers
programme.desc: Claytons Towing are under a lot of pressure when they have to salvage a yacht that has washed ashore. GRS Towing are on a 48-hour mission in the mountains. Wagga Truck Towing are working on a time-critical mission.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Polars top dog Todd Dewey gets into trouble with a government contract; truck problems have Polar driver Art Burkes season headed for the gutter; ice road rookie Mike Simmons has his first solo delivery.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: On the M11, Sean, Merrie and Dan come to the aid of a truck that has been left marooned by the collapse of a rear axle. Meanwhile, Alpha recovery set out to deal with a motorhome gutted by fire.

programme.title: Trucking Hell

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: The casualty department treats a broken leg from a forklift, a debilitating neurological disease, a possible spinal infection, and an injury from a fall.

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: The flood of funds into sculptural projects during the burgeoning British Empire unleashed a new, yet remarkably unorthodox, golden age. The greatest sculptors of the 18th and 19th centuries were mavericks who bucked prevailing trends - geniuses like John Flaxman, Francis Chantrey and Alfred Gilbert. Sooke reveals the technical breakthroughs behind their key works.

programme.title: British Historys Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy debunks the `Glorious Revolution. In 1688, the British Isles were invaded by a huge army led by Dutch prince, William of Orange. With his English wife Mary he stole the throne from Marys father, the Catholic King James II. This was the death knell for absolute royal power and laid the foundations of our constitutional monarchy.

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: The scam starts with a small repair, then escalates into major work that will supposedly save your home - but its really funding a lavish life for the scammers. Their chief weapon is Irish charm: the travelling conmen have been crossing America, convincing homeowners to part with millions of dollars. With the FBI on their case, Conor Spackman follows the money trail and lifts the lid on where the cash goes.

programme.desc: After discovering a long-held secret, Yasmin and Henry have to decide what their next move is. Meanwhile, Whitneys strategy with Tender is to keep on expanding. At SternTao, Harper comes clean to Sweetpea and Kwabena about a decision she made to keep the business afloat.

programme.title: Reporting Scotland

programme.title: The Harbour
programme.desc: Abnormally high winds create dangerous conditions which result in a rare closure of the harbour. Boatmen Alan Cowper takes his daughter to try out for Scotlands under-15 football team but faces an excruciating wait to hear whether she gets in. VTS controller Ralph Greig considers a career change, while out at sea saturation diver Terry Dearlove begins the lengthy process of decompression.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Dairy farmers Lucy and Adam risk it all with a huge financial gamble. Callums attempts to get lambs to market are hampered by storms, while Emma and Rob wrangle a feisty bullock.

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.sub-title: The Hunt for Arlene Frasers Killer
programme.desc: Police begin to piece together the clues after a mother vanishes from her home. What began as a missing person report quickly escalated into one of Scotlands most high-profile investigations.

programme.title: Scotland: The Promised Land
programme.desc: Examining the turbulent political situation that was emerging in Scotland, and the new era of class politics that was to materialise. The decade after the Great War would have a profound social and political impact on modern Scotland. Returning troops and exhausted industrial workers were promised a land fit for heroes, but instead found widespread poverty and revolution on the Clyde.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotlands biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: David Wilsons Crime Files
programme.desc: David travels to Edinburgh to investigate the racist murder of Ahmed Abuukar Shekh in 1989. It was a crime that challenged Scotlands view that racism didnt exist here, and to this day, no-one has ever been convicted of Ahmeds murder. In the studio, David meets Mukami McCrum, a former member of protest group the Lothian Black Forum, who fought to have Ahmeds murder recognised as racist.

programme.desc: The fierce Drag Race UK spin-off continues as our international queens fight to impress Mama Ru and do their countries proud. Phasers set to stun! The queens become Space Girls as they tackle the iconic girl group challenge - but with an inter-gay-lactic twist. Drag Race superfan and chart topper RagnBone Man drops in to give the queens a masterclass in vocals, while girl group legends Bananarama join RuPaul, Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel.

programme.title: Alan Carrs Picture Slam
programme.desc: This week, Alan welcomes three new teams to take on the picture challenge: co-parents Sas and David from Haywards Heath, flatmates George and Joe from Manchester and father-son duo Sanjay and Kavi from east London. Theyll face each other across four frantic rounds against the clock - including the brand new Picture Twist and buzzer rounds - with one team making it to the final Super Slam. Will they know their Idris Elba from their peach melba and take home the £10,000 jackpot?

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The fierce Drag Race UK spin-off continues as our international queens fight to impress Mama Ru and do their countries proud. Silence is golden as the queens tackle an acting challenge with a difference. Split into two teams, the queens must let their bodies do the talking as they star in silent movies, with direction from Doctor Who supremo Russell T Davies. Acting superstar Will Poulter joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Alan Carr. May the best drag queen win!

programme.title: Fall
programme.desc: After Dan dies climbing the Rocky Mountains, his girlfriend Becky and her friend Hunter decide to climb a huge 1,000-foot communications tower and dump Dans ashes.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: James May is taken far outside his comfort zone as he straps into an insanely fast, highly tuned, 600 horsepower hatchback and is thrown into the lions den of World Rallycross racing. Nicholas Hoult tries his hand in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Robbie Savage: Making Macclesfield FC
programme.desc: After Macclesfield Town Football Club is wound up in September 2020, former Premier League star-turned-pundit Robbie Savage and local businessman Rob Smethurst attempt to build a new club from the ashes: Macclesfield FC.

programme.title: Robbie Savage: Managing Macclesfield FC
programme.desc: Directors box to dugout - Robbie Savage has officially entered his football manager era at Macclesfield FC, despite swearing it was a role he never wanted. But the allure of coaching at the club he helped create from the ground up with businessman best friend Rob Smethurst is just too strong.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps Sarah from Newcastle upon Tyne swap city bustle for Northumberland bliss. Shes after peace, privacy and a new family home with views to lift the soul, which one of houses delivers in style. Jules also takes Sarah to explore the glorious walled garden at Capheaton Hall, blooming with colour and countryside charm.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a rural special, Christina Trevanion delves into farming-related antiques and collectibles as she finds out which red and blue teams milked the most profits at auction.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: In Provence, the markets and shops are awash with an extraordinary array of fresh fruit, all locally produced. Its got Marcus thinking, so he sets out to see whether the French also have a passion for retro recipes.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus wants to give the French a run for their money with a new take on Britains most popular Sunday lunch: roast chicken. But first, he needs the perfect poulet, so he visits local farmer Alex to check out the local poultry.

programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn invites a panel of guests to share their opinions on the newsmaking stories of the day.

programme.title: !mpossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz show where players face a question and pick from a board of six rankable answers. Some answers are good, but only one is unbeatable.

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz show where being in control is everything. Who will emerge as the Boss and walk away with the cash? Only the strongest players will survive the game.

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: In Pezenas, in the neighbouring region of Occitanie, Viki has her eye on some pottery. Meanwhile, Ian thinks a pair of jugs featuring a duck and a cockerel will fly off the shelves.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers the story of the monks and villagers of the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, off the Northumberland coast. From Holy Island, monks spread Christianity to the rest of Northern England and created one of Britains finest treasures: The Lindisfarne Gospels.

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The team investigate why parts of the Southern Ocean are warming twice as fast as the rest of the worlds oceans, and take a look at the impact of this phenomenon. They also go in search of the weedy sea dragon, and explore kelp forests to investigate how theyre threatened. Plus, the team dive one of the thousand shipwrecks in these waters, and in a sunken valley, they search for mysterious deep ocean creatures.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Presented from the Royal Marines Museum at Southsea in Hampshire. The building started life as a barracks for Royal Marines, and since 1864 thousands have been trained and served there. Antiques expert Charles Hanson uncovers a navigational tool and Catherine Southon enthuses about a decorative Japanese cabinet dating back to the 1870s. Meanwhile, Paul Martin finds out what it was like to serve as a Royal Marine Commando.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Michelle Ackerley, Katie Derham, Karen Hauer and Geoff Norcott test their general knowledge skills on this weeks Richard Osmans House of Games: Redemption Week.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: The quiz heads to Cardiff as Vernon Kay, with Paul Gorton in tow, tests Anneka Rice, Shane Todd and Remi Burgz on their UK knowledge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North West England: Starter and Fish
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for north west England with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British movies. Who will be eliminated?

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The HS2 High Speed Rail line from London to Birmingham has kept archaeologists busy for several years, but few sites have been as puzzling as the ruin of a Norman church just outside Stoke Mandeville.

programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts as QI goes voyaging with Nabil Abdulrashid, Joe Lycett, Lou Sanders and Alan Davies.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Join the CBBC crew for all your favourite programmes. Tune into CBBC every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Its almost time for the ice cream van to pay its daily visit and Bitzer has his heart set on a well-earned treat. However, his work is not done - theres a whole list of chores standing in his way and he needs his wits about him to get the job done in time.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: DM must save his reputation and the world when Squawkenclucks artificially intelligent defence super-computer goes insane.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Much to Trevors irritation, a rare phoenix woodpecker takes a liking to him. He demands an immediate quest to return the bird to its real home.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: When Cheese accidentally begins writing her new fantasy novel inside Boys diary, Boy is inspired to live out the life of the novels great hero! By applying the story to his life, Boy is soon one of the cool kids, making Girl jealous. When she sees Cheeses novel-writing is the cause, Girl helps her write the next chapter to take Boy down a peg or two.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy has played for too many hours on the console. His eyes are so fatigued that he cannot stand a single ray of light. The Lemmings choose this very moment to throw a big birthday party with as many lights as possible.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Karen gets a computer virus, and it is up to SpongeBob to try and get it out of her.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Olive goes undercover in a high-stakes Rock, Paper, Scissors game.

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: Someone has been eating the cookies in the cafe, and there is a rodent-shaped hole in reception. Thinking that there is a rat in the leisure centre, Danny and Mick put a plan into action to catch the illusive rodent.

programme.sub-title: You Could Live in Slow Motion?
programme.desc: Find out why living in slow motion would let you see things no-one else can!

programme.desc: Learn about animal and plant adaptation, inheritance and the theory of evolution.

Duggee reminisces about his radio days running Clubhouse Radio, playing music, telling jokes, chatting to callers and reading the news. It was the most popular show on air, until a new rival show appeared and replaced him: the tellingly-named Cheese Radio.

programme.title: The Game Catchers

programme.desc: Kyra and Khloe play at being a fairytale queen and princess, but when Khloe eats a pretend magic apple, she falls into a deep sleep. Later, they enjoy a walk in the park with Mum, and there is more pretend play, with leaf monsters and crocodiles. Back at home, Kyra and Khloe become chefs and help Mum make a special dinner for the whole family.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Choekyi has been shown a traditional way to make momos, which are a type of dumpling. First, Choekyi makes dough that is then rolled out into circles. She fills these circles with a mixture of spinach, cheese and mushrooms. When they tried mushrooms at first, Choekyis friends did not really like the taste or smell. Lets hope they prefer them when they are cooked in the momos. Choekyis recipe comes from Tibet, so when her friends arrive she greets them in a traditional Tibetan way.

programme.desc: Therapy dog Toffee and his owner Helen help Betty and Pop-Pop feel brave enough to perform at their talent show at the Woofton Care Home.

programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet some dholes. These are wild dogs that live across Asia, from China to Thailand, as well as in the dense forests of India. Next, Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet dwarf mongooses. They can live in groups of up to 30 and are tiny.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle isnt allowed at school, so hes being looked after by Connie, the Brooklyn-Bells neighbour, at her house. programme.title: Ghulam-E-Musthafa
programme.desc: When Dayanand Dixit refuses to sell his property to Verma, Ghulam, a gangster and hitman, is asked to move into their house, live with them, and eventually force them to leave.

programme.title: Professor
programme.desc: Sita devi is a very strict aunt for a number of young ladies, and does not allow them to fraternise with males. Along comes Pritam disguised as an elderly Professor Khanna to break every possible rule of the aunt.

programme.title: Khal-Naaikaa
programme.desc: After her husband kills himself, an embittered pregnant widow loses her child, and embarks on a mission of vengeance against a woman and her family.

programme.title: John Day
programme.desc: A robbery at his branch gives a bank manager a lead to catch those responsible for his daughters death.

programme.title: Dear Zindagi
programme.desc: An unconventional thinker helps a budding cinematographer gain a new perspective on life.

programme.title: Jab Pyaar Kisise Hota Hai
programme.desc: A Playboys past catches up with him when he wants to get married.

programme.title: Lootcase
programme.desc: Nandan Kumar comes across a suitcase and discovers it is full of cash. He knows nothing about who it belongs to, where its from, or how it got there. Nandan has to decide whether or not to take it or leave it.

programme.title: Phir Hera Pheri
programme.desc: Greed for more and easy money takes the rags-to-riches men Baburao, Shyam and Raju back to bankruptcy.

programme.title: Naqaab
programme.desc: Naqaab brings together director duo Abbas-Mastan with another thriller. The focus of the film shifts to the shady past of all the characters, with some unexpected elements thrown in.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is thrilled when his name is used in a `Soap Opera Digest crossword puzzle. He is reluctant to let the magazine do a profile of him, but Rachel tries to persuade him to reconsider so that he can mention her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby Shower
programme.desc: Rachel panics when she realises how unprepared she is to care for an infant. She reluctantly agrees to allow her frustrating mother Sandra to move in to help her, upsetting Ross. Meanwhile, Monica tries to atone for forgetting to invite Sandra to the baby shower, and Chandler and Ross exhaust themselves by repeatedly playing a confusing game called Bamboozle to help Joey prepare to audition as a game show host.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Cooking Class
programme.desc: When Monicas cooking at the restaurant receives a harsh write-up in a newspaper, she goes to a criticism class taught by the reviewer, hoping to convince him to reconsider his opinion. Meanwhile, Rachel gets upset when a cute salesgirl flirts with Ross while he and Rachel are shopping for baby supplies, and nervous about a major job interview, Chandler receives some questionable coaching from Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Is Late
programme.desc: When Rachels pregnancy goes beyond the expected delivery date, the pain makes her increasingly irritable and willing to try anything that might speed up the babys arrival. Meanwhile, Monica and Phoebe bet on the date of the birth, while Joey can only take one guest to the premiere of his film, and chooses Chandler. Unfortunately, Chandler falls asleep during the screening.

programme.title: Criminally Funny
programme.desc: Featuring a robber on a roof, offenders in an office, and a criminal in a canal.

programme.title: Criminally Funny

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Poker
programme.desc: When the girls lose money to the guys in a poker match they seek out Monicas Aunt Iris, a famous card player, for lessons in poker and demand a rematch.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where the Monkey Gets Away
programme.desc: Rachel accidentally lets Ross pet monkey escape, and the friends form a search party. She is then horrified to learn that her former best friend is engaged to marry her ex-fiance.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Evil Orthodontist
programme.desc: Rachel gets back together with her ex-fiance Barry, even though hes engaged to her best friend Mindy. Meanwhile, Chandler agonises over a wonderful woman who fails to call him after their first date.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Fake Monica
programme.desc: Someone is pretending to be Monica, using her credit card numbers and having the sort of fun that Monica wishes she could have.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ick Factor
programme.desc: Monica discovers that shes developed a highly mature approach to her latest love affair, and Ross finds out that hes being mistaken for a male prostitute.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Carol gives birth, Ross and Susan almost miss the event when Phoebe accidentally locks them in a cupboard together to put a stop to their constant bickering. Meanwhile, Joey befriends an unmarried pregnant woman and ends up helping her to give birth.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Finds Out
programme.desc: Ross has been in love with Rachel since ninth grade but has always kept it a secret. Now the truth accidentally comes out, but as Rachel rushes to the airport to greet Ross on his return from a business trip, she discovers that work is not all hes been getting up to while hes been away.

programme.title: Shanghai Knights
programme.desc: Anachronistic sequel to `Shanghai Noon. Two friends travel to 19th-century London to take back the Imperial Seal of China, which has been stolen by an evil English lord. Along the way they meet Jack the Ripper, Sir Arthur Conan Doyle and a young Charlie Chaplin, and also find time to indulge in a little kung-fu fighting.

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: The Brand New Tour
programme.desc: Kevin Bridges returns to the state to give his take on social media, anxiety, conversations with his dog, and having Barack Obama as his support act.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts this skit comedy show where the actors on the show, usually Wayne Brady, Colin Mochrie, Ryan Stiles and another guest star or two do different comedy skits. Its all improv and made up on the spot.

programme.desc: The World Canadian Bureaus leader forces the country into a painful strike and the boys try desperately to fix the situation.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Ms Garrison wants to become a man again, so whilst she is away Cartman becomes the substitue teacher. Its not long before Cartman is teaching innercity kids the fun of cheating.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The citizens of South Park find out that suddenly internet is gone. When Randy hears that there could be some internet out in California, he packs up with his family and heads west.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Ed Staffords Rite of Passage
programme.desc: Ed visits the Hamar cattle herders of Ethiopia to witness a unique rite of passage. The tribes young men must successfully jump over a line of bulls to earn their status and be recognised as men.

programme.desc: Gordon visits family-owned restaurant Sams Mediterranean Kabob Room in Monrovia, California. Within minutes of his arrival, Gordon is caught in the crossfire of an argument between owner Sam, his wife and their children, all of whom are feeling the pressure of working in the restaurant seven days a week.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Long Beach, California, to save Ninos, an Italian family restaurant that was opened in 1958 and is now under the misguided leadership of the eldest son, Nino. Younger brother Michael and sister Carina point the finger of blame for the restaurants failings directly at Nino, and their 78-year-old mother Inge is desperate for Nino to turn the business around.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Misty and Eric have outgrown their DIY disaster of a home. With three daughters and one on the way, they search for a larger home to fit the needs of their growing family.

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: The crew has the day off and even Capt. Glenn recharges by enjoying Parsifal III all to himself and riding the eFoil; Colin saves the day clearing the clogged toilets, but gets jammed up when his honest opinion strikes a chord.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Joe Thomas struggles with the definition of a mammal, Iain Stirling devises an explosive chemistry experiment and Sian Gibson sends an NSFW text to Greg.

programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts with guest captain David Walliams and Jonathan Ross going up against Jon Richardson and Joe Lycett. Isy Suttie joins Countdowns lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: Green Wing

programme.desc: The Tattoo Fixers come to the aid of Patrice, whose tattoo portrait of her dearly departed grandmother ended up looking like a well-known Corrie star. Cheeky Michael needs help with a holiday tattoo thats writing cheques his anatomy cant cash.

programme.desc: With the McQueen house in lockdown, original family members return, as a series of what if scenarios play out during a high-stakes situation. And John Paul makes a life-changing decision.

Before boarding he meets schoolteacher Jean Hewlett, and is captivated. He plans romance, unaware that she already has a sweetheart: Dewey Connors, commander of the Corsair and Stewarts boss. Will their rivalry be discovered in time?

programme.title: Crash Dive
programme.desc: Set aboard the USS Corsair, a submarine on active duty. Against his wishes, Ward Stewart is made executive officer and given just days to prepare. Before boarding he meets schoolteacher Jean Hewlett, and is captivated. He plans romance, unaware that she already has a sweetheart: Dewey Connors, commander of the Corsair and Stewarts boss. Will their rivalry be discovered in time?

programme.title: Twelve OClock High
programme.desc: "Were in a war, a shooting war. Weve got to fight, and some of us have got to die". Frank Savage is the WWII commander drawing every ounce of fight from his weary airmen.

programme.title: Django Unchained Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Quentin Tarantino and some of his cool cast talk about their exciting new western adventure, `Django Unchained.

programme.title: First Knight
programme.desc: Dissatisfied with King Arthurs rule of peace and freedom, Malagant, an erstwhile ally, turns against the Round Table and establishes a hostile domain. However, he goes too far when he kidnaps the queen and imprisons her in an appalling dungeon. The special skills of the glittering and impetuous swordsman Lancelot must be called upon to effect her rescue.

programme.title: Men in Black International Interview Special

programme.title: BlackBerry

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieris caught up in a whirlwind of flavours. In Norfolk, VA, he finds a two-for-one spot where a butcher stacks sandwiches by day and a chef fires up ramen and sticky wings by night.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs representing Northern Ireland go head to head. Judging as ever are Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined by Shehan Hettiaratchy, lead trauma surgeon at St Marys Hospital who helped treat victims of the Grenfell Tower fire.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs from all 8 regions must now battle it out for the honour of a spot on 2018s `Feast To Say Thank You banquet menu, celebrating 70 years of the NHS, one of Britains most prized institutions.Each day the regional champions will be cooking one of their courses again for the discerning panel of judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but after some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the starter round, everyone is desperate to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. Once again the chefs present their innovative, witty or heart warming creations, with lobster, mackerel, John Dory, turbot and hake among the dishes being scrutinized.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. Both chefs and judges alike are amazed at the high standard of cooking and an incredible number of 10s are dished out as the chefs serve up venison, rabbit, duck, beef, lamb and pork for scrutiny.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and with just one place remaining at the banquet feast, each chef is giving everything theyve got for a chance to say thank you to the heroic NHS staff. All the chefs have significantly re-worked or re-invented their desserts since the regional heats, proving that theyll do everything they can in a bid for that final winning spot.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale as the winning chefs cook their dishes at a banquet for the heroes of the NHS. Having arrived at St Bartholomews Hospital, the chefs are shown the incredible location for event, the almost three hundred year old Great Hall.

programme.title: The Real Oppenheimer
programme.desc: In 1945, the world was ushered into the atomic age. With his role as the Father of the Bomb solidified, Oppenheimer became increasingly worried about the potential danger that scientific inventions could pose to humanity.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: For four decades two global superpowers face off, poised for swift and terrible action. It is an arms race, a propaganda battle and a deadly game. It is the Cold War.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: As the arms race rages on, the 1950s become a decade of paranoia. The threat of mutually assured destruction maintains the stalemate between democracy and communism.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: Russia seals itself behind an Iron Curtain, spawning the birth of revolutions across East Europe as the bitter rivalry between capitalism and communism rages from Berlin to Havana and all the way to Outer Space.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: The stakes couldnt be higher in the 60s as the USSR places nuclear warheads in Cuba, the first US troops arrive in Vietnam, and the world witnesses the moon landing.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: By the 1970s Stalin is long gone, but Russias determination strengthens. Advances in nuclear technology have poured fuel on the fire in the international arms race.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: By the end of the 70s, war between East and West has been raging for decades. Countless proxy wars have proven how destructive this so-called Cold War can be. Its an arms race, a propaganda battle and at times a deadly game.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: In the early 1980s the next battlefield in the struggle between communism and democracy, emerges in the United States own backyard. The Cold War is an arms race, a propaganda battle and at times a deadly game.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: In 1989, the USSR pulls out of Afghanistan and the last remaining proxy conflict of the Cold War ends. A new era emerges as brutal barriers are broken down. A peaceful transition is what the watching world prays for, but is by no means a sure thing.

programme.title: Oppenheimer
programme.desc: Robert Oppenheimer leads the effort to develop the atom bomb during the Second World War. When revealed, his work initially makes him a hero in the United States, but a changing world soon sees him recast as a security risk.

programme.title: The Blitz on The North
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on how the German Luftwaffe and flying bombs caused devastation and chaos across the north of England and parts of Scotland.

programme.title: The Blitz On The Midlands
programme.desc: A documentary assessing the effect of the concerted German bombing of cities and towns in the midlands during World War Two.

programme.title: Hitler vs Stalin
programme.desc: Historians analyse the Battle for Stalingrad, focusing on Hitler, Stalin, and Zhukovs campaigns.

programme.title: Hitler vs Stalin
programme.desc: Historians analyse the Battle for Stalingrad, focusing on Hitler, Stalin, and Zhukovs campaigns.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Germany took just four weeks to conquer Poland, using a new form of warfare - Blitzkrieg. Six armoured tank units known as Panzer divisions, supported by the Luftwaffe, quickly broke through Polish defences.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: After the retreat from Dunkirk, Churchill rallied Britain in its darkest hour. Hitler was only just across the channel, but he reckoned without our supreme strategist, the might of the navy and the manoeuvrability of the air force.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: By the 1970s Stalin is long gone, but Russias determination strengthens. Advances in nuclear technology have poured fuel on the fire in the international arms race.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: By the end of the 70s, war between East and West has been raging for decades. Countless proxy wars have proven how destructive this so-called Cold War can be. Its an arms race, a propaganda battle and at times a deadly game.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: In the early 1980s the next battlefield in the struggle between communism and democracy, emerges in the United States own backyard. The Cold War is an arms race, a propaganda battle and at times a deadly game.

programme.title: M.A.D. World

programme.title: M.A.D. World

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A team discovers clues to Scotlands first kingdoms, metal detectorists unearth a hoard of Viking treasure, and a new housing development reveals a graveyard of Iron Age warriors.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: River Hunters sees Rick and river-searching expert Beau searching the unexplored rivers and waterways of Britain, unearthing treasures from the past which reveal how our various ancestors used to live.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: River Hunters sees Rick and river-searching expert Beau searching the unexplored rivers and waterways of Britain, unearthing treasures from the past which reveal how our various ancestors used to live.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick and river-searching expert Beau head to Salisbury and explore the River Avon.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The team find evidence from one of Scotlands bloodiest eras as they wade through the waters of the River Garry in the Scottish Highlands, the site of the first major clash between Highland Jacobites and the government army.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: In medieval England, the North was ruled from York. Dan Jones explores the history of its castle, from the Viking Jorvik and the Normans, through the dark days of the anti-Jewish pogrom and the imprisonment of the Knights Templar, to one of its most colourful inmates - the legendary highwayman Dick Turpin.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: With the aid of dramatic reconstruction, Dan Jones explores the 900-year history of the beautiful Leeds Castle in Kent, from its origins as a royal Saxon familys manor and Norman stronghold to the private property of six medieval queens, a Tudor palace, a Jacobean country house, a Georgian mansion and an elegant 20th-century retreat for the rich and famous.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The British Win Every War?
programme.desc: What did the Romans ever do for us? Well, they certainly put the Brits through their paces back in AD 43. Al is joined by Caesar Bruno Tonioli to look at key battle tactics during the Roman Invasion. From wading through the waves in Roman armour, to seeing how hard it was to invade, to the power struggle the Celts went through to hold onto their identity and just how difficult it is to fire a catapult.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The British Win Every War?
programme.desc: Al faces up to one of the worlds superpowers and the richest nation on Earth to discover how the former British colony of America somehow slipped through its fingers. Joining him to fight for his nations honour is comedian Reginald D Hunter.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Finds include the lost WWI training trenches on Salisbury Plain, Britains first `double henge, discovered just down the road from Stonehenge, where the evidence suggests our ancestors feasted and made sacred offerings as part of a visit to the ritualistic Stonehenge landscape, and luxury foreign goods discovered at Tintagel, the legendary childhood home of King Arthur.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team is in the north of Britain where they uncover evidence of the first Roman siege in Britain, Scottish man-made islands and a famous monastery.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The team find evidence from one of Scotlands bloodiest eras as they wade through the waters of the River Garry in the Scottish Highlands, the site of the first major clash between Highland Jacobites and the government army.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick Edwards and Beau Ouimette embark upon an epic search through the River Ouse, which winds its way through North Yorkshire, in a bid to find evidence that it was once home to Viking life.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Edinburghs handsome hilltop bastion is Scotlands most popular paid tourist attraction. With a slew of sieges to its name, it is no surprise that the site boasts some formidable defences, and Dan puts one heavy duty cannon through its paces.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the history of Cardiff castle over more than 2,000 years and reveals how it became transformed from a strategically important Roman and Norman stronghold to a lavish fairy-tale Victorian home.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes 45 miles through the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the areas rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At the impressively preserved Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps Tony relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. Tony finishes in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series Tony takes on a tough four-day trek through the Kintail region of the west Scottish Highlands to discover the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s. On three occasions, Highland armies, assisted by the French and the Spanish, attempted to overthrow the King and put a Stuart back on the throne.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts follows the excavation of Iron Age Britains most spectacular grave. A team of archaeologists in East Yorkshire have uncovered the remains of only the third upright chariot burial ever found in Britain.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores 2019s most exciting archaeological finds.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The British Win Every War?
programme.desc: Its 865 AD and the Great Heathen Army, the Vikings, have invaded Britain. Al takes up his sword to discover the truth behind how the Vikings were so successful in Britain. With John Thomson, they explore the truth of what Vikings really looked like.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The Brits Win Every War?
programme.desc: Al meets the Auld Enemy, where bitter sibling rivalry is as strong today as its ever been. Joined by Sanjeev Kohli, Al visits Craufurdland Castle to explore the truth behind famous battles from the Wars of Scottish Independence.

programme.title: Truthseekers
programme.desc: Hundreds of years ago, a small group of Polynesians rowed across the open sea, navigating by the stars. A look at why they left their native land.

programme.title: Truthseekers
programme.desc: Scientists, historians and archaeologists attempt to unravel the greatest mysteries of history.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: In the late 18th century, there was a sure-fire way to earn a living along the Cornish coast: smuggling. The great walking country is home to tiny secretive harbours, beaches and secluded coves which were once ideal for the infamous illicit imports.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Setting off from Liverpool, Tony takes a three-day walk inland to Wigan, tracing the story of the Leeds and Liverpool Canals chaotic construction and its dramatic impact on the development of Lancashire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a grand five-bedroom period house in Folkestone in Kent, a two bedroom flat in Londons Leytonstone and a two bedroom mid terrace in Fenton in Stoke on Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A studio flat in a former 1960s office block which is grade two listed in Londons Elephant and Castle, a terraced house in Pontygwaith in South Wales and a flat in in Cradley Heath near Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with no floor in Edgbaston in Birmingham, a detached house on several levels in Oldham and a former garage complex ripe for development in Forest Hill in London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A converted 16th century chapel in Matlock in Derbyshire, a two bedroom flat in Whetstone in London and a water damaged house in Wishaw in Scotland are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two bed semi in Meir in Stoke on Trent is given a country house look, a flat in Londons Willesden Green also gets the wow factor and a semi in Dudley in the West Midlands loses its 1980s look to be brought into the 21st century.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Terraced houses in Gravesend in Kent and Shipley in Derbyshire are given a new lease of life while former church premises in Harrow Weald in Middlesex are given a new use for the community.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion meet property developers who have all bought at auction and hope to make a profit on their investments. Today a landscape gardener tackles a terrace in Ilkeston in Derby, two friends take on a four storey house in Sittingbourne in Kent while another couple of friends first venture together is a studio flat in South Norwood in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a stone cottage in Tilton on the Hill in rural Leicestershire while Martin Roberts is at a property auction buy in Croydon on the outskirts of London and Lucy Alexander meets two friends who have bought a bungalow in Rainham in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Chaddesdon in Derby, a two-bedroom flat in Londons Maida Vale and a property in Warrington are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in Londons Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin tackles the issue of solar panels on a property in Mountain Ash in Wales and wonders if itll be a sunny outcome for the new owner, while Lucy looks round a barn in Elstree in Hertfordshire and Dion visits a flat in Newport in Shropshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple who buy a former millworkers cottage in Derbyshire as their first home together; Lucy Alexander meets a father and daughter team who are seasoned developers buying a house in Kent; and Dion Dublin talks to a man who, along with two friends, has clubbed together to buy an investment property in Lowestoft in Suffolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with a bathroom layout challenge in Ripley, Derbyshire, a huge three-storey house with its freehold on offer in Erdington, Birmingham, and a mid-terrace house in Londons East Ham in need of a major makeover are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Lincolnshire looking round a pair of rural semi-detached cottages which the new owner may convert into one home. Martin Roberts is in Cheshire at a bungalow with bags of potential, and Lucy Alexander is in Kent talking to friends embarking on their first project together.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house in Chesterton in Staffordshire has a smelly problem and needs a total overhaul. The new owner of a semi in Bestwood in Nottingham also has a lot to do, while the new owner of a flat in Strawberry Hill has less than a week to sort out her auction buy before she jets off to Germany.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A husband and wife duo add their design skills to a house in Croydon in Surrey, while a seasoned developer creates a high-end house in multiple occupation in Coventry and a first time developer also does a great job with a house full of period features in Nutall in Nottinghamshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in Londons Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has a hair-raising experience at a barbers in Rochdale, Lancashire, while Martin Roberts looks round a great bungalow in Alsager, Cheshire, and Lucy Alexander sees all the potential in a piece of land in Eccles in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Online Reviews: Can You Trust Them?
programme.desc: Whether shopping online, choosing a service provider or booking a holiday, online reviews can play a crucial role in guiding our spending decisions - businesses know that good reviews mean big business, but new research found that in some sectors, half of the online reviews analysed were flagged as suspicious. New laws promise to curb fraudulent feedback, but experts warn that it is a cat-and-mouse game to detect illegal postings which are still being used by scammers.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: When the body of a young drug addict is found in a flooded public lavatory, it appears that he is the victim of an overdose. Dr McKenzie, summoned from a dinner engagement, sees no reason to question this and finds himself in an invidious position when the post-mortem reveals that the young man was murdered. DI Frost is called upon to solve the mystery.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When a local industrial dispute threatens to get out of hand, Millers personal feelings almost get the better of him and Rachel is called in to investigate. Meanwhile, David becomes a source of inspiration for a famous musician and Peggy sees an opportunity.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.sub-title: Out of the Long Dark Night
programme.desc: When vandals target a local couple, Joe investigates and uncovers a dark history, while Peggy gets her hands on a ton of scrap metal - but how to shift it is a problem.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When the wife of a wealthy landowner is bludgeoned to death with a cricket bat, DCI Tom Barnaby and his team are enmeshed in a succession of calculating murders. Once again, blackmail, deceit, greed and revenge threaten to destroy Midsomers tranquillity.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: While a group of animal welfare activists launch a dangerous campaign, Carol and Dr Chris clash over the care of a pregnant teenager, and Peggy is pleased as punch when she thinks David has caught a certain somebodys eye.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When a teenage girl disappears, the police have to spring into action, and Peggy thinks theyve struck gold when David finds an old metal detector.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When a popular and well-loved member of a coastal community is violently and deliberately run off the road, DCI Vera Stanhope must investigate the victims past if she is to stand any chance of finding their killer.

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The `Inspector Morse prequel `Endeavour won such big ratings when it aired on ITV in 2012, the network is giving fans more in the form of six film-length episodes. The series follows the relationship - both working and personal - between young DC Endeavour Morse and his senior partner, DI Fred Thursday. Returning actors include Shaun Evans as Endeavour and Roger Allam as Thursday, while Sean Rigby takes on the role of a young PC Strange - the character who appeared in the original `Morse series, played by James Grout - and Abigail Thaw, the daughter of the first Inspector Morse, John Thaw, make further guest appearances as Dorothea Frazil.

programme.title: Trial and Retribution
programme.desc: When a notorious crime family shoots dead a shop manager during a jewellery raid, an accomplice turns informer, only to be gunned down himself. As a witness to the slaying, DS Satchell finds his life and that of his young daughter in danger, and nowhere appears to offer sanctuary from the malign influence of the crime family - not even the police station itself.

programme.title: Trial and Retribution
programme.desc: Conclusion of a two-part story. With his wife and child missing, a frantic DS Satchell is desperate to resolve the situation, whatever it takes. Walker and Connor uncover a previously hidden pressure point in the Bilkin family, and begin to close down the evidence to finally put the family away. However, Satchell, feeling betrayed and helpless, has already decided to take matters into his own hands.

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited

programme.desc: Series following members of the M-Sport WRC team about the trials and tribulations of competing in Rally driving at the highest level.

programme.title: FIM Superbike World Championship Motorcycle Racing
programme.desc: The 2026 World Superbike Championship begins with the Australian Round - the 4.445km track at the Phillip Island Grand Prix Circuit will push the riders to their very limits as they look to get their first points of the season on the board. The beautiful circuit on the Bass Strait off of the coast of Victoria in Australia is one of the most iconic and exciting venues in world motorsport - WorldSBK first raced at Phillip Island in 1990 just after the first MotoGP visit in 1989.

programme.desc: Lisa follows Jack southbound on the Dalton, Hugh faces white-outs and bumpy roads on his return trip to Fairbanks, Alex completes his first solo trip and rescue teams scramble to retrieve an overturned truck.

programme.sub-title: Jimmy Snuka and the Death of Nancy Argentino
programme.desc: Investigating the mysterious story of Jimmy Snuka, whose girlfriend Nancy Argentino died in their motel room in 1983, and revealing her familys 30-year struggle for justice.

programme.desc: During a weekend in the countryside, the lads meet a fellow Brit who is apparently in trouble.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Based on a true story, fugitive Sam Sheppard is found guilty of killing his wife despite his pleas of innocence.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: A body is hauled out of the English Channel, however it is still very much alive and raising a family.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: A highly regarded officer is suspected in the disappearance of several soldiers.

programme.title: Police, Camera, Murder
programme.desc: Unprecedented access reveals the critical role of digital forensics in modern murder investigations. A schoolboy is found stabbed in the street and an elderly pensioner has been tortured to death - can smartphone and CCTV analysis solve the crimes?

programme.title: Police: Suspect No. 1
programme.desc: In Norfolk, an otherwise unassuming summer evening is shaken by an armed robbery. A vehicle is stolen and innocent people are shot.

programme.title: Police: Suspect No. 1
programme.desc: Detective Inspector Burgess of Norwich CID has a problem as the number of home burglaries has rocketed. Looking at the evidence, officers suspect that one man is potentially responsible for a large part of the spike in residential crime.

programme.title: Police: Suspect No. 1
programme.desc: After a raid on a cannabis factory, nine people are arrested and it is up to the team to get to the bottom of which role each suspect played.

programme.title: Police: Suspect No. 1
programme.desc: The team in Norwich gather evidence about a single-punch attack that has left a man seriously injured, and there are no willing witnesses.

programme.title: The Boneyard
programme.desc: Chilling documentary charting a brutal real life tale of kidnapping, enslavement and murder. Find out how investigators tracked down a malicious murderer and explore the extensive trial that followed.

programme.title: Killed Walking Home
programme.desc: Every three days, a woman is killed in the UK. These women could be sisters, daughters and friends. Although there is a lead that breaks open the case, what comes in light of the investigation is one that leaves an everlasting scar on the families.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: A mother of two is charged after placing a 911 call stating her two sons have been stabbed by an intruder.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Woman found dead and her husband who was drunk that evening confesses to her murder but none of the evidence points to him.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Ted Binion at the Las Vegas casino tells his lawyer to remove his girlfriends Sandys name from his the will if she doesnt kill him tonight.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: After a series of murders, French police are racing to catch the Beast of the Bastille. They rely on DNA in a case that would create the French DNA database of sexual offenders.

programme.title: Mansons Missing Victims
programme.desc: Stories about people who disappeared when they attempted to leave the Barker Ranch.

programme.title: FBI: Criminal Pursuit
programme.desc: A predator in a white truck abducts a 10-year-old girl as she rollerblades in front of her house. Will FBI agents be able to find Pamela before its too late?

programme.title: FBI: Criminal Pursuit
programme.desc: Teenage mum Rachel elopes with a mystery man and two weeks later her body is found chained and weighted in a lake. Who murdered her, and is her baby still alive?

programme.title: FBI: Criminal Pursuit
programme.desc: After small town family man Andie is murdered at a shopping centre, the case goes unsolved for 15 years. Can the FBI unravel this sordid tale of adultery and greed?

programme.title: FBI: Criminal Pursuit
programme.desc: When nine-year-old Jimmy Ryce is abducted at the bus stop, a local psychic claims to have visions of his fate; who has taken him and will he be found alive?

programme.title: Jack the Ripper: Londons Most Notorious Killer
programme.desc: Unmasking Jack the Ripper and his evil deeds with a presentation of both factual evidence and conspiracy theories.

programme.title: Aileen: Life and Death of a Serial Killer
programme.desc: This feature-length film examines Aileens harrowing life, tracing her story from the freezing woods of Michigan to the steamy swamps of Florida, and features her closest friends, her lawyers and even her estranged birth mother. At the heart of the film are the astonishing, unique, troubled interviews Broomfield filmed with Wuornos in prison, including her last, defiant words before execution.

programme.title: Killed Walking Home
programme.desc: Every three days, a woman is killed in the UK. These women could be sisters, daughters and friends. Although there is a lead that breaks open the case, what comes in light of the investigation is one that leaves an everlasting scar on the families.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: A mother of two is charged after placing a 911 call stating her two sons have been stabbed by an intruder.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Woman found dead and her husband who was drunk that evening confesses to her murder but none of the evidence points to him.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Woman found dead and her husband who was drunk that evening confesses to her murder but none of the evidence points to him.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Ted Binion at the Las Vegas casino tells his lawyer to remove his girlfriends Sandys name from his the will if she doesnt kill him tonight.

programme.title: The Secret Lives of Big Cats
programme.sub-title: The Secret Lives of Snow Leopards
programme.desc: An examination of the fierce snow leopard and how it became legendary for both its rarity and elusiveness.

programme.title: Cheetah: Beating the Odds
programme.desc: A cheetah raises her young family on the vast expanse of the Serengeti, facing enormous challenges. Lions are quick to throw their muscle around, and hyenas and jackals attempt to steal anything the cheetah manages to kill.

programme.title: Chandani: The Daughter of the Elephant Whisperer
programme.desc: Chandani has a burning desire to follow in her fathers footsteps and become a mahout. A Mahout takes care about elephants.

programme.title: Antarctica Dreaming: Wildlife on Ice
programme.desc: Antarctica Dreaming documents all major Antarctic and sub-Antarctic animals.

programme.title: In the Land of the Moose
programme.desc: At the end of the summer, a moose finds herself alone in the forest. She has just lost her calf, probably to a bear. Now she must find a mate. Once pregnant again, she will endure months of bone-chilling winter until she delivers in the spring.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Getting to know a comparatively unknown corner of Switzerland, the Jura region. The film is a journey of discovery into almost untouched nature in the change of the seasons. The Jura Mountains are the little brother of the Alps.

programme.title: Otters: River Masters of Yellowstone
programme.desc: Follows two families of river otters in Yellowstone and how they join together to face challenges.

programme.title: Wild About New Zealand
programme.desc: Aoraki/Mount Cook houses New Zealands highest mountain and its longest glacier, Tasman Glacier, making it a hotspot for mountaineering, ski touring and scenic flights.

programme.title: Wild About New Zealand
programme.desc: Whanganui includes areas of Crown land, state forest and many former reserves; New Zealands first national park, Tongariro, is recognised as a World Heritage Site with several sacred Maori places and three active volcanoes.

programme.title: The Trackers Diary: Bears of Katmai
programme.desc: Every fall in the northern Pacific, up to a million salmon migrate back to the rivers and streams where they were born to spawn. Many of these streams are located in Katmai National Park, Alaska.

programme.title: The Trackers Diary: Bears of Katmai
programme.desc: Everyone hates biting insects, but if people were to look a little closer, they would be able to see that even the tiniest and most irritating bug can be essential for a working ecosystem.

programme.title: The Gentle Bear Man of Emo
programme.desc: Michael finds that the bonds he forges with the bears and other wild animals that live on his property in Emo, Ontario, helped heal his soul after a tragedy. His relationship with the animals put him on a path to love and redemption.

programme.title: Of Shark and Man
programme.desc: "Of Shark and Man" is a ground breaking film about one mans journey to get closer than anyone thought possible, to the worlds biggest Bull Sharks and tell the incredible untold story of Shark Reef in Fiji, one of the greatest marine conservation successes of all time. A love letter to the worlds most feared predator, in which the shark is the hero.

programme.title: Hoanib - The Secrets of The Desert Elephants
programme.desc: The Namib is one of the most hostile deserts, but some animals have developed ways to survive.

programme.title: Mystery Monkeys Of Shangri-La
programme.desc: A thrilling tale of courage, instinct, desperation and hope.

programme.title: Jaguar: King of the Jungle
programme.desc: The jaguar is the third largest of the big cats, and pound for pound, the most powerful. This jaguar patrols a home range of over 100 square kilometers in Brazil, deep in the heart of the worlds largest tropical rainforest.

programme.title: The Secret Lives of Big Cats
programme.desc: An examination of pumas, which are distantly related to lions and much more closely related to cheetahs.

programme.title: The Secret Lives of Big Cats
programme.desc: An examination of lions, which are highly social and form prides of up to 35 animals, unlike other cats.

programme.title: The Secret Lives of Big Cats
programme.desc: An examination of leopards, which like most big cats, are elusive and need to be virtually invisible to hunt.

programme.title: Coral Sea Dreaming
programme.desc: The filmmaker highlights the survival strategies of marine creatures in a coral, which is an underwater ecosystem characterised by reef-building.

programme.title: Blue Water Savages
programme.desc: Robin and Eli use this episode to show you the uneasy relationship between spear fishermen and sharks.

programme.title: Wild About New Zealand
programme.desc: Aoraki/Mount Cook houses New Zealands highest mountain and its longest glacier, Tasman Glacier, making it a hotspot for mountaineering, ski touring and scenic flights.

programme.title: Wild About New Zealand
programme.desc: Whanganui includes areas of Crown land, state forest and many former reserves; New Zealands first national park, Tongariro, is recognised as a World Heritage Site with several sacred Maori places and three active volcanoes.

programme.title: The Trackers Diary: Bears of Katmai

programme.title: The Trackers Diary: Bears of Katmai

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Its not surprising that Sue Perkins finds plenty of humour in her family tree, even in the poignant stories of an orphaned grandfather and a great-grandfather interned as an enemy alien during the First World War. But when she delves into the German branch of her family, who were living in eastern Europe through the Second World War, she uncovers a harrowing tale of refugees fleeing back and forth across borders between Nazi and Soviet control.

Recruiting a new group of thrill-seeking cohorts, Xander finds himself entangled in a deadly conspiracy that points to collusion at the highest levels of government.

programme.title: Old Guy
programme.desc: An ageing hit man must train a young prodigy when his employer moves to replace the old guard. However, when they learn they are being betrayed, the unlikely pair turns into a lethal team, with their double-crossing bosses in their sights.

programme.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
programme.desc: The Good is Blondie, a wandering gunman with a strong personal sense of honour; the Bad is Angel Eyes, a sadistic hit man who always hits his mark; and the Ugly is Tuco, a Mexican bandit whos always only looking out for himself. Against the backdrop of the Civil War, they search for a fortune in gold buried in a graveyard. Each knows only a portion of the golds exact location, so they must work together.

programme.title: Wrath of the Titans
programme.desc: Perseus enlists the aid of Queen Andromeda, Hephaestus and Poseidons son to rescue Zeus from the underworld, defeat the Titans and save mankind.

programme.desc: Weather scientists Jo and Bill put marital problems aside to track a slew of devastating tornadoes raging through Oklahoma and stay one step ahead of their better-equipped but arrogant rivals. They have to get to the tornado first to be able to test a radical new device, by inserting it directly into the heart of the storm.

programme.title: Mr. Nice Guy
programme.desc: Martial arts romp about a TV chef who becomes embroiled in a drug lords search for an incriminating video. programme.title: The LEGO Batman Movie
programme.desc: There are big changes brewing in Gotham, but if Batman wants to save the city from the Jokers hostile takeover, he may have to drop the lone vigilante thing, try to work with others and maybe, just maybe, learn to lighten up.

programme.title: The Blues Brothers

programme.title: Its Complicated
programme.desc: When attending their sons college graduation, a couple reignite the spark in their relationship. However, the complication lies in the fact that they are divorced and he has remarried.

programme.title: The Intern
